name: Chilean Cobalt Corp.
business: Chilean Cobalt Corp. is a critical minerals exploration and development company focused on the La Cobaltera and El Cofre cobalt-copper projects in northern Chile. The company has not yet generated revenues and is in early stages, planning to produce cobalt and copper concentrates and/or intermediates for various end-products.

exposures.sentence: Our growth depends upon the continued growth in demand for end-products utilizing rechargeable storage batteries, particularly electric vehicles.
Our growth depends upon the continued growth in demand for end-products utilizing rechargeable storage batteries, particularly electric vehicles.

We are a critical minerals exploration and development company focused on the La Cobaltera and El Cofre cobalt-copper projects, located in the San Juan District in northern Chile, one of the world's few known primary cobalt districts.

We anticipate producing cobalt and copper concentrates and/or intermediates for application in a diverse range of end-products, including electric vehicle batteries and for a wide variety of industrial, pharmaceutical, aerospace, electronic and polymer applications.

On January 8, 2026, we entered into a binding earn-in and option agreement with NeoRe SpA, a privately-held Chilean company to acquire approximately 6,300 hectares of mining concessions (the Properties) within the coastal belt region near Concepcion Chile with an ionic adsorption clay-style rare earth elements system enriched with yttrium, neodymium, dysprosium and terbium elements critical to defense and advanced manufacturing supply chains.

Cobalt demand has been driven by the growth of its use in high performance metal alloy products for industrial and defense applications, as well as in lithium-ion batteries for portable electronic devices and electric vehicles.

We anticipate producing cobalt and copper concentrates and/or intermediates for application in a diverse range of end-products, including electric vehicle batteries and for a wide variety of industrial, pharmaceutical, aerospace, electronic and polymer applications.

catalysts.event: Signed Deed of Undertaking with Glencore for right of first refusal on cobalt and copper product
catalysts.sentence: On November 11, 2025, we signed a Deed of Undertaking with a subsidiary of Glencore plc ( Glencore ) whereby Glencore has been granted a right of first and last refusal to purchase cobalt and copper product from the La Cobaltera and El Cofre projects, which it expects to ship to the United States or U.S. Free Trade Agreement countries.

catalysts.event: Extended non-binding LOI with USSM for downstream processing
catalysts.sentence: On September 6, 2024, and then extended on September 5, 2025, we put in place a non-binding LOI with USSM to process and refine cobalt and copper concentrate we expect to produce.

catalysts.event: Completed first independent Digbee ESG assessment
catalysts.sentence: In 2025, the Board approved the adoption of the Digbee and IRMA ESG frameworks, and we completed our first independent Digbee ESG assessment in July 2025.

catalysts.event: Board adoption in principle of new governance framework
catalysts.sentence: We continue to strengthen our governance and ESG systems, including the Board's adoption in principle of a new governance framework in March 2026, which is intended to support enhanced oversight, disclosure readiness, and our consideration of an uplisting to a national securities exchange in 2026.

catalysts.event: Binding earn-in and option agreement with NeoRe for rare earth project
catalysts.sentence: On January 8, 2026, we entered into a binding earn-in and option agreement with NeoRe SpA, a privately-held Chilean company to acquire approximately 6,300 hectares of mining concessions (the Properties) within the coastal belt region near Concepcion Chile with an ionic adsorption clay-style rare earth elements system enriched with yttrium, neodymium, dysprosium and terbium elements critical to defense and advanced manufacturing supply chains.
